name: Bislama
notes: Bislama — English-lexifier creole (national language of Vanuatu), with an Oceanic (Austronesian) phonology and grammar. Post-1995 standard orthography: 5 vowels, the digraphs ⟨ae⟩ and ⟨ao⟩ for diphthongs, ⟨ng⟩ /ŋ/. The pre-1995 spelling used ⟨é⟩, ⟨ai⟩/⟨au⟩ and macron-marked ⟨m̄⟩ /mʷ/, ⟨p̄⟩ /pʷ/; labialised consonants are now written with a following ⟨w⟩ (mw, pw) — labialisation is not modelled here. Spelling is broadly phonemic. Some speakers have a few Oceanic-substrate phonemes (/pʷ/, /mʷ/) not present for all.

sources.author: Crowley, Terry
sources.title: Bislama Reference Grammar
sources.notes: Reference grammar; phonology and the standard orthography.

sources.author: Holm, John
sources.title: Pidgins and Creoles, Volume II: Reference Survey
sources.notes: Reference survey of Atlantic and Pacific pidgins/creoles; lexifier and substrate attributions.

sources.author: Michaelis, S.M., Maurer, P., Haspelmath, M. & Huber, M. (eds.)
sources.title: The Atlas of Pidgin and Creole Language Structures (APiCS)
sources.notes: Structural atlas of 76 pidgins/creoles; per-language survey chapters cited in ancestry notes.

ancestors.notes: English lexifier (~90% of the lexicon); Bislama is an English-lexifier Melanesian pidgin/creole. Holm (1989:529-535); APiCS ch. 20 (Meyerhoff).

Central/Northern Vanuatu Oceanic (Malayo-Polynesian) substrate: 5-vowel system, (C)V syllable preference, transitive suffix -em reflecting the Oceanic transitive *-i. The specific substrate languages (e.g. North Efate, Nguna) are unregistered; nearest registered node is Malayo-Polynesian. Crowley (2004); APiCS ch. 20.
French adstrate from the Anglo-French Condominium period (e.g. pikinini and other lexical items).
